test_cases.title: Skip an individual step marks it as skipped without completing it
preconditions: User is authenticated and onboarding wizard is active
User is on a step that has not yet been completed
The onboarding wizard has at least two steps
steps: Navigate to an incomplete step in the onboarding wizard
Click or activate the 'Skip' option for that individual step
Observe the step's status in the wizard progress or state
test_cases.expected_result: The step is marked as 'skipped' (not 'completed'), and the wizard advances without treating the step as successfully finished

test_cases.title: Skip the entire wizard dismisses it permanently for the user account
preconditions: User is authenticated and onboarding wizard is active
User is at any point during the onboarding flow
steps: Click or activate the 'Skip entire wizard' option at any step in the onboarding wizard
Confirm the dismissal if a confirmation prompt appears
Observe that the wizard is dismissed
test_cases.expected_result: The onboarding wizard is dismissed immediately and the user is taken to the main application; the wizard is permanently dismissed for this user account

test_cases.title: Wizard does not reappear on subsequent logins after being skipped entirely
preconditions: User has previously skipped the entire onboarding wizard
User has logged out of the application
steps: Log back in to the application with the same user account
Observe the application state after login
test_cases.expected_result: The onboarding wizard does not appear on login and the user lands directly in the main application interface

test_cases.title: User has immediate full access to all system features after skipping the entire wizard
preconditions: User has just skipped the entire onboarding wizard
The application has navigated away from the wizard
steps: Attempt to access at least three distinct system features immediately after skipping the wizard (e.g., dashboard, settings, core functionality)
Observe whether each feature is accessible without restriction or additional prompts to complete onboarding
test_cases.expected_result: All system features are immediately accessible without any onboarding-related restrictions, banners blocking access, or forced redirects back to the wizard

test_cases.title: User has immediate full access to all system features after skipping individual steps
preconditions: User is authenticated and onboarding wizard is active
User has skipped one or more individual steps without completing the wizard normally
steps: Skip at least one individual step in the onboarding wizard
Complete or exit the wizard through normal flow after skipping
Attempt to access system features that might be associated with the skipped steps
test_cases.expected_result: All system features are accessible without restriction regardless of which steps were skipped

test_cases.title: Wizard skip persistence is scoped to the user account and does not affect other accounts
preconditions: User A has skipped the entire onboarding wizard
User B exists and has not yet encountered the onboarding wizard
steps: Log out of User A's account
Log in with User B's credentials
Observe whether the onboarding wizard appears for User B
test_cases.expected_result: The onboarding wizard appears normally for User B; the skip state from User A's account does not affect User B's onboarding experience

assumptions: A 'Skip' control exists for both individual steps and the entire wizard in the UI, though the exact label or mechanism is not specified in the requirement
The onboarding wizard is shown to users who have not yet completed or dismissed it on first login
The permanent dismissal state is stored server-side per user account, not in browser local storage or cookies alone
There is no mandatory prerequisite that blocks feature access until onboarding is completed — full access is unconditional after skipping
Individual step skipping is available on each step independently, not only at the start of the wizard
notes: The requirement does not specify whether a confirmation dialog should appear when skipping the entire wizard; no such test case was added to avoid inventing behavior. It is unclear whether skipped individual steps can be revisited or completed later — no test covers that scenario. The requirement does not define what 'full access' means in terms of specific features, making exhaustive access testing ambiguous. There is no stated behavior for what happens if a user skips some steps and completes the rest — specifically, whether the wizard is then considered 'done' or still 'partially skipped'. The requirement does not address whether skipped steps are surfaced anywhere in user profile or settings for later completion.
